current_fact.fact_text: On this day in 2000, Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on mission STS-101 to supply and repair the still-young International Space Station. The crew installed new gear, boosted the station’s orbit, and helped turn a bare orbital frame into a livable outpost.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

current_fact.match_reason: A supply flight weaving between Earth and an orbiting outpost parallels a DJ journey that slowly ‘builds’ a landscape, layer by layer, into a full, living groove.

queue.fact_text: In 1996, Space Shuttle Endeavour flew mission STS-77, an experimental flight that deployed free-flying satellites and even tested inflatable structures in orbit. It was a laboratory for ideas about how future spacecraft might unfold, drift, and work autonomously in space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #25 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: An experimental shuttle mission testing new orbital concepts echoes trip-hop’s experimental spirit, floating between genres the way satellites drift between orbits.

queue.fact_text: In 1971, the Soviet Union launched Mars 2, an ambitious mission that included both an orbiter and a lander. The lander crashed during descent, but the orbiter sent back the first images of Mars from a Soviet spacecraft, revealing a world of vast canyons and ancient craters.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: Plunging a spacecraft into the Martian atmosphere—and crashing—mirrors the episode’s metaphor of immersion and the risks of diving below the surface to discover something new.

queue.fact_text: On 19 May 1845, Sir John Franklin’s expedition departed England, seeking a fabled Northwest Passage through the Arctic ice. Both ships vanished into the polar night, leaving behind frozen clues that fascinated—and haunted—explorers for generations.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: Both the Franklin expedition and this episode move into the Arctic circle; one in wooden ships, one in a wetsuit, both chasing mystery in cold, beautiful, dangerous waters.

queue.fact_text: In 1962, Madison Square Garden hosted a birthday tribute to U.S. President John F. Kennedy, immortalized by Marilyn Monroe’s breathy, theatrical rendition of "Happy Birthday." Her performance lasted barely a minute, yet became one of the most iconic—and mythologized—live songs in political history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

queue.match_reason: Monroe’s legendary birthday song is an early example of a woman’s voice reshaping a public moment, aligning with an episode that honors powerful, iconic female vocalists.

queue.fact_text: In 1997, Mexico’s Sierra Gorda region—home to cloud forests, deserts, canyons, and over 2,300 plant species—was declared a biosphere reserve. Remarkably, the protection emerged from grassroots activism led by local communities rather than top-down decree.

episode.title: Morning Drops #29 - Morning Dubs
episode.description: Recorded while still processing Houghton Festival. One of the mornings there, waking up in the woods to a reggae DJ set — no phone signal, just trees, music, and loved ones. A slow selection of reggae, dub, and morning rhythms. Dubs instead of drops. Start your day slow — with space to breathe.
songs: The Gaylads - Joy in the Morning
King Tubby - King Tubby Meets the Rockers Uptown

queue.match_reason: A forested, biodiversity-rich region saved by local people fits the earthy, tree-surrounded, roots-driven mood of waking up to dub in the woods.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1535, Jacques Cartier set off on his second voyage to North America with three ships and Chief Donnacona’s two sons—whom he had kidnapped on his first expedition. The journey deepened French knowledge of the St. Lawrence River, but also intensified a fraught and unequal encounter between Europeans and Indigenous peoples.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: A sea voyage of exploration and cultural collision pairs with an episode about travel, landscapes, and stories carried by guests—modern, reflective counterparts to historic journeys.

queue.fact_text: In 1798, Napoleon Bonaparte sailed from France to invade Egypt, bringing not only soldiers but also more than 150 scientists and scholars. Their studies led to the discovery of the Rosetta Stone and helped found modern Egyptology, turning a military campaign into an accidental scientific expedition.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: Napoleon’s Egyptian campaign—military on the surface, transformative in knowledge—mirrors travel as a moving meditation that unexpectedly shifts perspective and culture.

queue.fact_text: After the fierce 1942 Battle of the Coral Sea, the U.S. Navy’s Task Force 16 turned toward Pearl Harbor for urgent repairs. The battered carriers and crews would soon return to sea and play a decisive role in the Battle of Midway just weeks later.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: Warships limping home between storms of battle echo the episode’s theme of weathering hard times, repairing, and holding on to the belief that it ‘can’t rain forever.’

queue.fact_text: On 19 May 1776, a Continental Army garrison surrendered at the Battle of The Cedars near Montreal after a series of skirmishes and miscommunications. The small conflict became a cautionary tale about overextension and the fragile logistics of a revolutionary army far from home.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A Revolutionary War defeat rooted in resistance and miscalculation fits with an episode about political rage, protest, and the high cost of fighting power.

queue.fact_text: In 1900, Great Britain formally annexed New Zealand’s tiny Tonga Island, a remote speck in Tasman Bay surrounded by clear waters and marine life. Though politically minor, it reflects how even the smallest islands became entangled in global imperial maps.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: An unassuming island absorbed into an empire pairs with an episode literally at sea, navigating small landfalls, maritime maps, and the quiet power of ocean journeys.

queue.fact_text: New England’s "Dark Day" of 1780 brought an eerie midday twilight over parts of the U.S. and Canada, so intense that people lit candles at noon. The cause—likely massive forest fires—was unknown at the time, fueling superstitions, doomsday fears, and whispered talk of the apocalypse.

episode.title: Morning Drops #10 - The One With Many Names
episode.description: Yasmin guides us through the glamorous world of Ghost, the Swedish band that turned rock into ritual. From masked clergy to shifting identities, diving into the music and myth of one of the most theatrical acts of our time.

queue.match_reason: A sudden, ominous daylight darkness and apocalyptic anxiety match Ghost’s theatrical, occult-tinged rock and their play with ritual, fear, and spectacle.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1933, Finnish cavalry officer C. G. E. Mannerheim was appointed field marshal, the highest rank in Finland. He would later become a national symbol for leading the country through the Winter War and World War II with a mix of stern discipline and careful restraint.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: Ascending to field marshal is a jolt in a military career; the high-voltage rock and blues of this episode resonate with martial energy, strategic tension, and forward drive.

queue.fact_text: In 1802, Napoleon Bonaparte founded the Legion of Honour, a new order of merit that rewarded talent and service rather than noble birth. Its red ribbon and star would become an enduring symbol of recognition, worn by artists, scientists, soldiers, and citizens alike.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: An order created to honor achievement parallels Sedna FM’s very first episode, a kind of founding act that sets the tone and ‘decorates’ the station’s future with its own badge of identity.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1743, French scientist Jean-Pierre Christin proposed a thermometer scale with 0° for the freezing point of water and 100° for boiling—the centigrade system we now know as Celsius. His simple inversion of an earlier design helped standardize temperature worldwide.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: A new way to measure heat and cold fits with an episode about temperature of mood—slowing the planetary pace, finding a gentle, calibrated climate for the day.

queue.fact_text: In 1922, the Soviet Union founded the Young Pioneer Organization, a mass youth movement that blended summer camps, science clubs, and ideological training. Millions of children wore red neckerchiefs, learned songs, and were taught to see themselves as builders of a new socialist future.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: A giant youth organization shaping identities through songs and slogans parallels an episode about young voices using rap to shape culture and values from below.

queue.fact_text: On 19 May 1911, Canada created the world’s first national park service, the Dominion Parks Branch—now Parks Canada. It marked a shift from exploiting wild landscapes to consciously protecting them for both nature and people.

episode.title: Morning Drops #21 - Endless Summer
episode.description: Recorded from Fano, a quiet town on the Adriatic coast where summer stretches long and slow. Between swims, simple meals, and time with family, Gaia reflects on the kind of music that helps us return to ourselves. Through different shades of jazz — from minimal and meditative to warm and rhythmic — an invitation to slip into that endless summer feeling.

queue.match_reason: Protecting national parks so people can rest in nature aligns with a sun-warmed, outdoorsy episode about long summers, slow time, and landscapes that hold you.

queue.fact_text: In 2016, EgyptAir Flight 804 vanished from radar over the Mediterranean Sea en route from Paris to Cairo, with all 66 people on board lost. The crash triggered an international search over deep water and a complex investigation into what had happened in the dark of the night sky.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A tragic, unresolved loss over the sea calls for a reflective, tender episode about vulnerability, recovery, and looking back at what changed you.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2007, a gunman in Moscow, Idaho, opened fire in a series of connected attacks that left three victims dead and several injured before he took his own life. The violence unfolded across ordinary, familiar spaces—homes, a church—shattering a small town’s sense of safety.

episode.title: Morning Drops #55 - JOMO
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores one of her own personality traits through the lens of psychology and music: the Joy of Missing Out. 

From the Need for Cognition to Deliberate Ignorance and Inattentional Blindness, the episode traces what it means to be someone whose brain demands depth before it gives energy, and what happens when the world around you expects the opposite.

Listen at your own pace, and let us know if you recognize yourself in this one.

Tracklist:
Burna Boy - Ye
2Pac - I Ain't Mad at Cha
Jet Life - Paper Habits
Travis Scott - Stop Trying to Be God
Andrea Laszlo De Simone - Il Regno Animale

queue.match_reason: A disturbing burst of public violence contrasts sharply with an episode about stepping back from overstimulation, suggesting a quiet, interior refuge when the world feels chaotic.

queue.fact_text: The Mediterranean Sea, over which Flight 804 was lost and Napoleon’s ships once sailed, is a nearly landlocked basin linking Europe, Asia, and Africa. Its waters preserve traces of Phoenician traders, Roman fleets, and modern migrants, making it one of Earth’s oldest and busiest cultural crossroads.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: An ode to the Mediterranean’s role as a cultural sea is perfectly paired with a musical ‘boat’ journey that sails between its coasts in song.

queue.fact_text: Tonga Island in New Zealand’s Abel Tasman National Park may be tiny, but its granite shores and clear waters shelter seals, seabirds, and kayakers resting between long paddles. Once annexed by an empire, it’s now best known as a quiet waypoint for people seeking wild, salt-bright silence.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: A quiet, sea-encircled island invites immersion in water and stillness, echoing an episode about floating, diving, and letting yourself sink into experience.

queue.fact_text: Finland’s Mannerheim would later go on to serve as president as well as field marshal, becoming an unusual figure who bridged military command and civilian leadership. His image—stern, mustached, and often on horseback—still looms large in Finnish memory and monuments.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'è Profondo il Mare

queue.match_reason: A leader straddling soft diplomacy and hard command matches an episode about contrasting energies—fog and fire—held together in one narrative.

queue.fact_text: Napoleon’s Legion of Honour, created in 1802, remains France’s highest order of merit today, awarded to astronauts, jazz musicians, and climate scientists alongside generals. A decoration born in war quietly evolved into a cross-field celebration of human achievement.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: An order that now honors artists as readily as soldiers pairs with an episode celebrating Afrobeat’s global cultural impact—another kind of ‘order of merit’ written in rhythm.

published.fact_text: In 1961, the Soviet probe Venera 1 became the first human-made object to fly by another planet, silently passing Venus after losing contact with Earth weeks earlier. Even without returning data, its lonely journey marked the beginning of interplanetary exploration beyond Mars.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A silent probe slipping past Venus fits the episode’s theme of reimagining emptiness as creative space and making a ‘space shift’ in perspective.

published.fact_text: On this day in 2000, Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on mission STS-101 to supply and repair the still-young International Space Station. The crew installed new gear, boosted the station’s orbit, and helped turn a bare orbital frame into a livable outpost.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: A supply flight weaving between Earth and an orbiting outpost parallels a DJ journey that slowly ‘builds’ a landscape, layer by layer, into a full, living groove.
